title: WATER-HISTORICAL-IMPORT
scope: USER-DIRECTED water domain, phase 4 of 12 — backfill historical water readings from a landlord's spreadsheet so the data foundation (86-89) feeds Phase-91 intelligence. BROWNFIELD: the import infrastructure ALREADY EXISTS — Import model + ImportStatus + ImportsController (/imports upload/index/show/reprocess/template, file mimes:csv,txt max10MB stored to local) + ImportService (CSV via ParsesCSVFiles str_getcsv; type-dispatch tenants|leases|water_readings|invoices|payments|units) + ImportPolicy (landlord+caretaker, immutable). There is ALREADY an importWaterReadings() — but with the exact bug the user warned about: it does NOT set is_invoiced=true (so imported history is billable by InvoiceService::calculateWaterCharges which bills approved+is_invoiced=false), does NOT set meter_id (Phase-86 made readings meter-keyed), has NO dedup (re-import duplicates), and the upload is CSV-only (user said EXCEL). PhpSpreadsheet (phpoffice/phpspreadsheet ^1.30) is used to READ xlsx in app/Imports/BankStatementImport.php (IOFactory::load(path)->getActiveSheet()->toArray()). WaterReadingObserver auto-recomputes consumption=current-previous + cost via tariff on create — for faithful HISTORY use Model::withoutEvents + explicit values. Meter::resolveActiveForUnit(unit) maps/creates the meter. Money decimal:2. SAFETY: imported readings = read-only history that must NEVER re-bill (is_invoiced=true) and re-import must be idempotent.

findings.title: Imported readings must be meter-linked and never re-bill
findings.evidence: importWaterReadings() omits meter_id and is_invoiced; an imported historical reading (status approved, is_invoiced=false) would be picked up by InvoiceService::calculateWaterCharges and billed again.
findings.remediation: In importWaterReadings: resolve Meter::resolveActiveForUnit(unit) -> meter_id; create the reading with status=approved + is_invoiced=true (read-only history, excluded from billing) + review_notes='Imported historical reading' for provenance + recorded_by=Auth::id(). Phase89 test: an imported reading has is_invoiced=true + meter_id set, and a generated invoice for that lease does NOT bill it.

findings.title: Preserve historical consumption/cost faithfully
findings.evidence: The observer recomputes consumption=current-previous and cost with TODAY's tariff, discarding the spreadsheet's historical values.
findings.remediation: Support optional consumption + cost columns. Create via Model::withoutEvents with explicit consumption (row ?? max(0,current-previous)) and cost (row ?? round(consumption * WaterRateService::getEffectiveRate(unit),2) as a best-effort estimate). Extend the water_readings CSV template headers (+ optional consumption, cost). Phase89 test: a row with an explicit cost preserves it; a row without falls back to consumption*rate.

findings.title: Idempotent re-import (skip duplicate meter+date)
findings.evidence: Re-uploading the same sheet creates duplicate readings, double-counting history.
findings.remediation: Before creating, skip a row whose (meter_id, reading_date) already exists; count it as skipped (not failed) and surface summary.skipped_duplicates. Phase89 test: importing the same row twice creates ONE reading; the second run reports it skipped.

findings.title: Accept + parse Excel (.xlsx/.xls) uploads
findings.evidence: The user keeps water records in Excel, but the import accepts mimes:csv,txt only and parses via str_getcsv.
findings.remediation: ImportsController upload validation accepts xlsx,xls; ImportService dispatches by extension — xlsx/xls parsed via PhpOffice\PhpSpreadsheet IOFactory::load(Storage path)->getActiveSheet()->toArray() with the same header-normalisation as CSV (mirror BankStatementImport); CSV path unchanged. Phase89 test: an .xlsx of water readings imports the same as CSV.

findings.title: Surface the historical import from the water hub
findings.evidence: The /imports feature exists but isn't discoverable from the water hub.
findings.remediation: Add a landlord-only quick-link from the water hub to the water-readings import (/imports?type=water_readings or the imports index) + i18n. nav-audit clean. Phase89 test: route reachable; link present for landlord.

findings.title: Phase 89 behavioral + surface tests
findings.evidence: The never-re-bill guarantee + dedup + xlsx need regression guards.
findings.remediation: Phase89WaterHistoricalImportTest (imported reading is_invoiced=true + meter_id + not re-billed by an invoice; dedup skips a repeat; xlsx parses; cost preserved/estimated) + Phase89 surface (import type registered, xlsx accepted, water template headers). Existing import + water + invoice suites stay green.

findings.title: Runbook + closeout + memory + multi-reviewer pass
findings.evidence: Import needs operator docs; per-phase review discipline.
findings.remediation: docs/runbooks/water.md historical-import section (template, never-re-bill, dedup, xlsx, client-ready note for Phase 94). Multi-reviewer read-only pass. Memory: phase89 plan + forward-path + pending-path. Name next = Phase 90 [WATER-ARREARS-ENFORCEMENT].
